steps.title: Find the scholars' defining logic
steps.content: The scholars argue: the Harlem Renaissance “continues to shape culture in the United States today,” therefore it “never really ended.” The key signal is this link — they define the movement as continuing based on its continuing influence, not on whether people were actively creating works in the 1930s onward.

steps.title: Match the logical claim
steps.content: Choice D states exactly this logic: if a movement's influence continues, then the movement itself continues (in some sense). The scholars are redefining what it means for a movement to “end” — they argue that ongoing cultural influence counts as the movement continuing. The other three either describe what makes movements influential (A), contradict the scholars' position (B), or raise an irrelevant distinction between literary and nonliterary works (C). That matches choice D — D is correct.
